_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to think about why changes happen and what happens because of those changes.

_source.w: We are learning to think about why changes happen and what happens because of those changes.
y: Understanding causes helps us see why things do not just happen by accident.
Thinking about consequences shows us that changes can be both good and not so good.
Learning about cause and effect helps us make better decisions in our own lives.
eg: Discussing why mobile phones were invented and how they changed the way people talk to each other.
Thinking about what happened when cars replaced horses — faster travel but also pollution.
Asking 'Why did people start using computers?' and 'What changed because of that?'
Talking about how a new playground was built because the old one was worn out.
Exploring how the invention of television changed what families did in the evening.
Discussing a change at school and whether the results were good, bad, or a mix of both.
